prompt: How to conduct comprehensive and accurate situational awareness of space targets in the cislunar space, and support the effectiveness of short-term cislunar space tracking and monitoring tasks?

  "executive_summary": {
    "direct_answer": "To achieve comprehensive and accurate situational awareness of space targets in cislunar space, an operator should build a layered hybrid enterprise rather than rely on any single sensor, orbit family, or command architecture. The strongest public evidence supports five design choices. First, use a hybrid observation layer: ground optical for breadth, passive RF for emitting targets, cooperative radiometrics and crosslinks for friendly missions, and a space-based optical layer in cislunar vantage regions to reduce Earth- and Moon-driven exclusion gaps [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[76]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Space-Based-Assets/Ewart.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf). Second, use orbit-determination and prediction methods that are explicitly built for multi-body dynamics, sparse short arcs, poor Earth-line observability, and maneuver sensitivity rather than treating cislunar motion as a simple extension of GEO practice [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[41]](https://cdcl.umd.edu/papers/aas21.pdf)[[44]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Dinh.pdf)[[45]](http://advspace.publicshare.s3.amazonaws.com/NRHO+OD+with+Simulated+DSN.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf). Third, separate surveillance, tracking, characterization, and custody maintenance as distinct operational functions because they demand different sensor mixes, revisit patterns, and estimator assumptions [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf). Fourth, fuse measurements in a hybrid architecture with centralized truth maintenance but distributed local tasking and onboard preprocessing to cope with latency, downlink limits, and short-lived visibility windows [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf)[[125]](https://www.darpa.mil/research/programs/tbd2)[[127]](https://advancedspace.com/afrl-cislunar-mission-progresses-through-key-milestones/). Fifth, optimize tasking around short-horizon decision support: turn propagated uncertainty into localized search tasks, prioritize information-rich viewing phases, and hand off rapidly between wide-field discovery sensors and narrow-field custody sensors [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[46]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Cislunar-SSA/Fahrner.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
    "why_this_improves_short_term_tracking": "Short-term cislunar tracking missions succeed when the architecture is optimized for reacquisition probability, short-horizon uncertainty containment, rapid sensor handoff, and resilience to geometry-driven outages. The causal logic is direct. Wide-field sensors reduce the probability of losing a target at reacquisition. Narrow-field, higher-sensitivity follow-up improves astrometric quality once the target is found. Passive RF or cooperative ranging collapses range and range-rate ambiguity that angles-only optical tracking leaves weakly observed. Multi-body, maneuver-aware filtering slows covariance underestimation in unstable or weakly observable regimes. Distributed tasking with local autonomy reduces the chance that a cue arrives too late to be useful. Together, those choices convert intermittent detections into executable monitoring timelines measured in hours to days rather than loosely bounded catalog awareness [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
    "brief_assumptions": [
      "State of public evidence as of May 8, 2026, with no assumption of access to classified performance data [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[100]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AFRL_CHPS_FS_0222.pdf)[[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/).",
      "Cislunar space is treated as the region beyond GEO through the Earth-Moon system, including transfer corridors, Lagrange regions, lunar orbit, and operational trajectories that use those regions [[40]](https://bidenwhitehouse.archives.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Cislunar-Implementation-Plan-Final.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf).",
      "The report covers spacecraft, inactive objects, boosters or transfer stages, debris where relevant, and natural/background objects that affect discrimination [[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[101]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/atr-2023-01267.pdf).",
      "The focus is operational effectiveness for short-term tracking and monitoring, not abstract full-domain omniscience [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf)."
    ],
    "key_takeaways": [
      "Ground-only observation is useful but insufficient for persistent cislunar custody because weather, daylight, atmospheric scatter, Earth rotation, and Sun or Moon exclusion zones create recurrent blind periods [[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[76]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Space-Based-Assets/Ewart.pdf)[[77]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Zimmer.pdf).",
      "Space-based sensing is necessary for near-continuous awareness in key cislunar regions, but no single orbit family or single sensor layer provides complete coverage; distributed, geometrically diverse assets are preferred [[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[76]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Space-Based-Assets/Ewart.pdf).",
      "Optical sensing is the baseline non-cooperative search modality, but it must be supplemented by passive RF, cooperative radiometrics, and crosslinks where available because angles-only tracking leaves range and range-rate weakly observed and uncertainty can grow rapidly in multi-body dynamics [[10]](https://deepspace.jpl.nasa.gov/dsndocs/810-005/210/210A.pdf)[[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[36]](https://descanso.jpl.nasa.gov/evolution/AAS_18-226.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
      "Cislunar orbit determination is harder than GEO or LEO SSA because many regimes are weakly observable from Earth, small maneuvers can create large downstream state changes, and uncertainty can become non-Gaussian over day-to-week gaps [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).",
      "The field has progressed materially since 2022: CAPSTONE validated cislunar navigation experiments in NRHO, Oracle-M reached major pre-launch milestones including a March 16-21, 2025 hot-fire, Oracle-Prime completed public design milestones, NASA moved LunaNet and LCRNS from concept toward interoperable baselines and commercial procurement, and DARPA TBD2 pushed onboard cislunar processing concepts [[2]](https://www.nasa.gov/smallspacecraft/capstone/)[[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[32]](https://www.ssc.spaceforce.mil/DesktopModules/ArticleCS/Print.aspx)[[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf)[[125]](https://www.darpa.mil/research/programs/tbd2)[[127]](https://advancedspace.com/afrl-cislunar-mission-progresses-through-key-milestones/)[[132]](https://advancedspace.com/capstone-30aug23-update/).",
      "Public evidence remains much stronger for cooperative navigation and architecture concepts than for routine non-cooperative surveillance of unknown cislunar objects; recommendations should therefore distinguish established capability from plausible but not yet operationally validated ambition [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[100]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AFRL_CHPS_FS_0222.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf)."
    ],
    "evidence_position": "The strongest public evidence comes from official NASA mission pages and NTRS documents, JPL and DSN technical material, official AFRL or SSC fact sheets and releases, ESA program pages, and targeted mission documentation. Quantitative cislunar surveillance claims are often simulation-based or derived from case studies such as Chang'e 5 rather than sustained operational monitoring of unknown uncooperative targets, so quantitative claims in this report are deliberately bounded to what public evidence supports [[2]](https://www.nasa.gov/smallspacecraft/capstone/)[[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[10]](https://deepspace.jpl.nasa.gov/dsndocs/810-005/210/210A.pdf)[[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[12]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AFRL_ORACLE-Family-Of-Systems_FS_240402.pdf)[[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[100]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AFRL_CHPS_FS_0222.pdf)[[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/).",
    "scope_limits": "This report uses public, web-accessible evidence available by May 8, 2026. It emphasizes operationally relevant civil, defense, academic, and commercial sources, but it does not assume access to classified performance data. Where evidence is sparse, especially for non-cooperative military cislunar surveillance, the report states limitations explicitly rather than inferring hidden capability [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[100]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AFRL_CHPS_FS_0222.pdf)[[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/)."
  },
  "problem_framing_assumptions_scope": {
    "narrative": "The practical problem is not generic SSA. It is how to maintain actionable awareness of spacecraft, debris, and other relevant objects across the Earth-Moon system, especially for short-term monitoring tasks such as transfer-phase support, reacquisition, anomaly response, custody maintenance, and warning timelines measured in hours to days. In cislunar space, the search volume expands dramatically beyond GEO, classical Earth-centered assumptions degrade, and communications plus geometry constraints make simple extensions of GEO or LEO workflows inadequate. Public defense and civil sources repeatedly make the scale difference concrete: cislunar targets may be at least 10 times farther than GEO objects, implying more than a 1,000 times expansion in coverage volume, and some low-energy transfer concepts extend to roughly 1.5 million km, or about five times the Earth-Moon distance [[12]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AFRL_ORACLE-Family-Of-Systems_FS_240402.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[40]](https://bidenwhitehouse.archives.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Cislunar-Implementation-Plan-Final.pdf)[[43]](https://user.eng.umd.edu/~ulukus/papers/journal/6G-at-1-over-6g.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[98]](https://www.jhuapl.edu/Content/documents/CislunarSecurityNationalTechnicalVision.pdf).",
    "what_comprehensive_and_accurate_means_operationally": [
      "Comprehensive does not mean simultaneous perfect visibility of the entire Earth-Moon volume. It means the architecture can discover, track, and maintain decision-relevant awareness across the target orbit regimes and mission classes that matter operationally, with explicit understanding of where blind spots remain [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
      "Accurate means state, identity, and uncertainty products are good enough to support the next decision: reacquisition, warning, tasking handoff, collision screening, rendezvous support, or anomaly triage. The required accuracy is therefore function-dependent rather than a single universal number [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf).",
      "For short-term missions, the critical metric is not catalog elegance but whether the next scheduled or opportunistic sensor can still find the target inside its field of regard with acceptable latency [[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)."
    ],
    "operational_evaluation_dimensions": [
      "Detection range or sensitivity, including apparent magnitude or SNR limits where available [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf).",
      "Revisit and latency, especially whether the next observation can arrive before uncertainty growth makes search impractical [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf).",
      "Angular and range accuracy, including whether the measurement type directly constrains range or only line of sight [[10]](https://deepspace.jpl.nasa.gov/dsndocs/810-005/210/210A.pdf)[[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[36]](https://descanso.jpl.nasa.gov/evolution/AAS_18-226.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf).",
      "Coverage continuity under exclusion zones, lunar glare, weather, daylight, Earth rotation, and communications outages [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[76]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Space-Based-Assets/Ewart.pdf)[[77]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Zimmer.pdf)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf).",
      "Custody quality, meaning both localization confidence and identity confidence across gaps, handoffs, and maneuvers [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)."
    ],

      {

        "statement": "Current state of public evidence as of May 8, 2026, with historical context only where needed.",
        "why_it_is_reasonable": "Major cislunar monitoring programs and standards changed materially after 2022, including Oracle restructuring, LunaNet and LCRNS baseline releases, commercial lunar relay procurement, and DARPA TBD2 [[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf)[[125]](https://www.darpa.mil/research/programs/tbd2)."

  "definition_of_cislunar_situational_awareness_and_operational_functions": {
    "narrative": "Operationally, cislunar situational awareness is the end-to-end ability to detect, identify, track, characterize, predict, and maintain custody of objects beyond GEO through the Earth-Moon system with enough timeliness and confidence to support safe operations, warning, and mission decision-making [[40]](https://bidenwhitehouse.archives.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Cislunar-Implementation-Plan-Final.pdf)[[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf). This is broader than a catalog alone. A useful cislunar awareness system must answer at least four questions: what is present, where is it now, where will it be over the next hours to days, and how certain are those answers [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf). In practice, those questions cannot be solved by one monolithic process. Discovery, precise tracking, behavior characterization, and custody maintenance are different technical problems because they depend on different observables, different revisit patterns, and different tolerance for ambiguity [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf).",
    "operational_functions_executed_analysis": [
      "Surveillance is a search problem. It values field of regard, sensitivity, geometric diversity, and robust discrimination against clutter more than exquisite single-look precision [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf).",
      "Tracking is an estimation problem. It values repeatable measurements, favorable geometry, and direct observability of weak state components, especially range and range-rate [[10]](https://deepspace.jpl.nasa.gov/dsndocs/810-005/210/210A.pdf)[[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[36]](https://descanso.jpl.nasa.gov/evolution/AAS_18-226.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf).",
      "Characterization is a feature-fusion problem. It depends on brightness behavior, photometric variation, RF emissions, and contextual metadata more than on orbital state alone [[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf).",
      "Custody maintenance is a continuity problem. It fails when either state uncertainty or identity uncertainty grows faster than the architecture can recover [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)."
    ],

        "dominant_measure_of_success": "Coverage fraction, search volume scanned, probability of detection, warning time.",

        "main_failure_modes": "Feature ambiguity, sparse observations, silent target behavior, limited spectral data, classification-sharing limits [[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)."
      },
      {
        "function": "Custody maintenance",
        "primary_purpose": "Sustain both localization and identity confidence through gaps and maneuvers.",
        "dominant_measure_of_success": "Track continuity, reacquisition probability, bounded search volume, confidence in unique label.",
        "best_sensor_bias": "Distributed hybrid network with tasking automation, feature-aided fusion, and maneuver-aware filtering [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf).",

      }
    ],
    "key_operational_point": "A system optimized only for surveillance will not necessarily deliver good short-term tracking, and a system optimized only for precision OD of cooperative spacecraft will not necessarily deliver discovery or custody of unknown objects. Cislunar awareness must therefore be designed as a layered set of functions, not a single monolithic pipeline [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf).",
    "function_to_design_implications": [
      "If the mission is discovery of unknown non-emitting targets, invest first in wide-field optical geometry and search scheduling, not in premium radiometrics that require cooperation [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf).",
      "If the mission is short-term support to a known active spacecraft, cooperative ranging, crosslinks, and passive RF often buy more operational value than more optical search area because they directly collapse the dominant uncertainty dimensions [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/).",
      "If the mission is custody of a maneuver-prone target, identity features and maneuver-aware process models matter as much as raw astrometric precision [[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)."
    ]

  "cislunar_environment_orbit_regimes_and_target_taxonomy": {
    "narrative": "Cislunar operations differ materially from near-Earth SSA because the environment is governed by Earth-Moon-Sun multi-body dynamics, not weak perturbations on nearly Keplerian Earth orbits. Public studies emphasize that the region includes orbit families and transfer trajectories whose observability, stability, and uncertainty growth differ sharply from GEO or LEO patterns [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[41]](https://cdcl.umd.edu/papers/aas21.pdf)[[44]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Dinh.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf). The average Earth-Moon distance is about 384,400 km, and AFRL notes cislunar objects may be at least ten times farther than GEO, implying a potential coverage volume over 1,000 times larger than the traditional GEO-focused domain [[12]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AFRL_ORACLE-Family-Of-Systems_FS_240402.pdf)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/). Some low-energy trajectories extend to about 1.5 million km, or roughly five times the Earth-Moon distance, which further expands the practical search volume and stretches cue-to-collection timelines [[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf).",
    "mechanism_chain_linking_environment_to_tracking_difficulty": [
      "Multi-body dynamics remove the convenience of simple two-body invariants. In cislunar tracking, lunar gravity cannot be treated as a minor perturbation, and CR3BP-like motion is non-integrable and strongly numerical, so short optical arcs leave a broader admissible state set than an equivalent near-Earth arc [[41]](https://cdcl.umd.edu/papers/aas21.pdf)[[44]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Dinh.pdf).",
      "Weak observability from Earth is geometry-driven, not just distance-driven. In a representative 9:2 L2 NRHO, the orbital plane is approximately perpendicular to the line of sight from Earth, so Earth-based range and range-rate do not directly measure the main orbital motion [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[45]](http://advspace.publicshare.s3.amazonaws.com/NRHO+OD+with+Simulated+DSN.pdf).",
      "Instability and phase sensitivity amplify small errors. In representative NRHO cases, radius varies from roughly 3,500 km at perilune to about 71,000 km at apolune, producing 2 to 3 orders of magnitude variation in experienced acceleration over one revolution and causing forecast quality to depend strongly on orbital phase and model fidelity [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[45]](http://advspace.publicshare.s3.amazonaws.com/NRHO+OD+with+Simulated+DSN.pdf).",
      "Lighting geometry and background sources turn observability into a time-varying problem. Solar phase angle, lunar scatter, Earthshine, and exclusion zones can make a target alternately bright, marginal, or effectively unobservable even when the orbit is well known [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf).",
      "Communications architecture feeds directly into awareness quality. Earth-Moon one-way light time is around 1.3 seconds, but the larger operational issue is intermittent contact and routing, which is why LunaNet and LCRNS emphasize relay, store-and-forward, and onboard preprocessing rather than assuming continuous direct Earth return [[43]](https://user.eng.umd.edu/~ulukus/papers/journal/6G-at-1-over-6g.pdf)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[119]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/testset-for-cis-lunar-communications-and-navigation.pdf)."
    ],
    "key_environmental_drivers": [
      "Weak Earth-only observability for some lunar and halo-family geometries, including NRHO, where the orbital plane is approximately perpendicular to Earth line of sight [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
      "Large changes in local acceleration within a single orbit, especially in NRHO, where radius can vary from about 3,500 km perilune to about 71,000 km apolune in representative cases [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
      "Strong phase-angle and glare effects from Sun-Earth-Moon geometry, making targets alternately bright and nearly undetectable [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf).",
      "Communications delays and intermittent connectivity, with Earth-Moon one-way light time around 1.3 seconds and additional routing or coverage constraints in practice [[43]](https://user.eng.umd.edu/~ulukus/papers/journal/6G-at-1-over-6g.pdf)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf).",
      "Sparse measurement cadence and non-Gaussian uncertainty growth during multi-day gaps [[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf)."
    ],

In practical terms, short-term support emphasizes bounded search regions, rapid state refresh, and timely dissemination over perfect long-run catalog completeness.",

  "observation_architecture_ground_space_hybrid_layers": {
    "narrative": "The public record strongly favors a hybrid architecture. Ground assets are valuable because they already exist, can be geographically distributed, and can contribute optical and passive RF observations, but they cannot provide uninterrupted cislunar awareness. Space-based sensors reduce atmospheric and some exclusion constraints and improve geometry near the Moon and Lagrange regions, but they add launch, navigation, communications, and sustainment complexity. The best-supported design is therefore a layered system in which ground assets provide breadth and surge capacity, while space-based assets close exclusion-driven gaps and support continuous custody in key regions [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[60]](https://conference.sdo.esoc.esa.int/proceedings/sdc9/paper/258/SDC9-paper258.pdf)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[76]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Space-Based-Assets/Ewart.pdf)[[77]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Zimmer.pdf).",
    "causal_design_logic": [
      "Ground optical is a good breadth layer because the infrastructure already exists and observer-state knowledge is strong, but the same fixed terrestrial geometry that makes observatory calibration easy also creates hard gaps from daylight, weather, and exclusion zones [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[76]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Space-Based-Assets/Ewart.pdf)[[77]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Zimmer.pdf).",
      "Space-based optical improves continuity because it eliminates atmosphere and Earth rotation from the observer side, but it introduces observer-state and timing dependence that must be solved with navigation, relay, or crosslink support [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[71]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Badura.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[107]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Cislunar-SSA/Block.pdf).",
      "Hybrid architectures outperform either extreme because they mix denial modes. Weather or daylight that removes optical observations from one site does not remove passive RF. Lunar-proximate exclusion that harms ground viewing can be mitigated by cislunar placement. Cooperative radiometrics can then tighten the track when the target is friendly [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)."
    ],

Cooperative radiometrics remain the highest-confidence precision source for friendly missions.   "detection_orbit_determination_tracking_prediction_and_custody_methods": {
    "narrative": "Cislunar OD and tracking require methods built for sparse angles-only data, poor geometry diversity, non-Keplerian dynamics, maneuvers, and uncertainty sets that can become multi-modal. Public studies repeatedly warn that simply extending Earth-orbit methods is inadequate [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf). The most important shift is from treating tracking as a single-state estimation problem to treating it as a hypothesis-management problem: initial optical detections often support several dynamically plausible states, and the task is to manage, discriminate, and schedule observations across that hypothesis set before it spreads beyond the next sensor opportunity [[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
    "orbit_determination_and_tracking_method_comparison_table": [
      {
        "method": "Classical two-body IOD",
        "best_use": "Fast initial rough estimates in Earth-dominated cases.",
        "strengths": "Simple and computationally cheap.",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Poor match to multi-body cislunar dynamics and very short optical arcs [[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
        "short_term_value": "Low by itself for cislunar non-cooperative tracking."
      },
      {
        "method": "Angles-only admissible-region or measurement-centric IOD",
        "best_use": "Initial hypothesis generation from sparse optical tracklets.",
        "strengths": "Designed for line-of-sight-only data; explicitly handles range and range-rate ambiguity [[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Can generate many hypotheses; sensitive to priors and measurement noise [[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
        "short_term_value": "High for initiating custody from weak optical detections."
      },
      {
        "method": "Machine-learning-assisted cislunar IOD, for example MCCLOD",
        "best_use": "Short-arc cislunar periodic-family cases where training captures dynamical structure.",
        "strengths": "Simulation studies show up to two orders of magnitude positional improvement over a classical two-body benchmark in tested cases [[15]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Ojeda-Romero.pdf).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Mostly simulation-based; external validity to broader traffic remains unproven [[15]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Ojeda-Romero.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
        "short_term_value": "Promising for rapid initial-state refinement when training priors are appropriate."
      },
      {
        "method": "Batch least squares or differential correction",
        "best_use": "Arc-based refinement when enough observations exist.",
        "strengths": "Good global consistency and established operational heritage [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Can be brittle with poor initial guesses, sparse data, or unmodeled maneuvers [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
        "short_term_value": "Useful after initial correlation, especially for cooperative or better-observed targets."
      },
      {
        "method": "Sequential square-root or information filters",
        "best_use": "Responsive updating under nonlinearity and ill-conditioned covariance.",
        "strengths": "Numerically robust relative to naive covariance forms [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Still vulnerable to model mismatch and maneuver surprise [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y).",
        "short_term_value": "Core method for real-time or near-real-time track maintenance."
      },
      {
        "method": "Gaussian-mixture or adaptive Bayesian filters",
        "best_use": "Multi-modal uncertainty, sparse observations, and non-detections.",
        "strengths": "Better than single-Gaussian filters when uncertainty becomes complex [[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Higher computational cost; much evidence remains simulation-based [[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
        "short_term_value": "Important for reacquisition and search-window management after gaps."
      },
      {
        "method": "Maneuver-aware filtering or process-noise inflation or control-estimation methods",
        "best_use": "Targets likely to stationkeep or maneuver near key epochs.",
        "strengths": "Helps preserve consistency and detect otherwise hidden small maneuvers [[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Requires good maneuver-window assumptions or adaptive logic [[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y).",
        "short_term_value": "Very high for active spacecraft custody maintenance."
      },
      {
        "method": "Feature-aided tracking and identity fusion",
        "best_use": "Maintaining unique target label when metric data alone become ambiguous.",
        "strengths": "Uses brightness, fluctuations, spectral or RF data to restore label confidence [[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf).",
        "cislunar_limitations": "Feature observability can be sparse and phenomenology-dependent [[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf).",
        "short_term_value": "Important for multi-target or deployment scenarios."
      }
    ],
    "core_mechanisms": [
      "Angles-only tracking is foundational but weakly observes range and range-rate; public work notes that three measurements are typically required for observability with angles-only, versus two if angle rates are also available [[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf).",
      "NRHO and related orbits are poorly observed from Earth because line-of-sight geometry is weak for the dominant motion components, which means nominally precise range or range-rate data can still leave important state dimensions weak [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
      "Short-term prediction quality depends heavily on measurement timing. In NRHO, pass phasing within the orbit may matter more than total tracking time because the dynamics near perilune are especially nonlinear and informative [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
      "Cislunar uncertainty can deform beyond the validity of a single Gaussian during multi-day gaps, so bounded local search and multiple-hypothesis logic become operationally important [[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)."
    ],
    "worked_example_1_chang_e5_ground_optical_tracking": {
      "purpose": "Demonstrate what ground optical can and cannot accomplish in short-term cislunar monitoring using a documented real case [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf).",
      "inputs": [
        "Target class: bright, known, translunar sample-return stack and booster components [[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf).",
        "Measurements: more than 1,200 astrometric observations from the Chang'e 5 campaign [[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf).",
        "Geometry: outbound leg favorable enough for lunar-distance optical observation; return leg strongly degraded by high solar phase angle [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)."
      ],
      "process": [
        "Step 1: use initial cueing and ground optical collection to build tracklets and a best-fit outbound trajectory [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf).",
        "Step 2: monitor propagated uncertainty and schedule follow-up when expected astrometric error grows beyond about 1 arcsec, converting state uncertainty into a practical re-observation rule [[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf).",
        "Step 3: continue optical custody across the outbound leg, including observations down to about 2 degrees from the Moon and tracking of the rocket body until about 3.5 hours before lunar impact [[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf).",
        "Step 4: reconstruct the outbound trajectory post hoc with 3-sigma uncertainty on the order of 200 km radial and tens of km in-track or cross-track [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf).",
        "Step 5: note failure boundary: the return leg became largely infeasible because solar phase angle made the target too difficult for the same optical approach [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)."
      ],
      "output_and_lesson": "This case shows that ground optical can provide useful short-term support for bright, well-cued translunar targets even near lunar distance, but the same case also shows why ground optical alone is not a complete cislunar awareness solution: performance depends strongly on target brightness and geometry, and unfavorable phase angle can end the tracking opportunity even when the target is operationally important [[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[18]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Furfaro.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)."
    },
    "worked_example_2_cooperative_nrho_tracking_logic": {
      "purpose": "Apply the report's method-selection framework to a friendly NRHO spacecraft [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/).",
      "inputs": [
        "Target class: active cooperative spacecraft in a 9:2 NRHO-like geometry [[1]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/citations/20240005978)[[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[45]](http://advspace.publicshare.s3.amazonaws.com/NRHO+OD+with+Simulated+DSN.pdf).",
        "Operational need: short-term tracking and monitoring over the next hours to days.",
        "Constraint: Earth-based line of sight poorly constrains the dominant orbital motion [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[45]](http://advspace.publicshare.s3.amazonaws.com/NRHO+OD+with+Simulated+DSN.pdf)."
      ],
      "method_selection": [
        "Initial decision: do not rely on Earth-based range and range-rate alone because the orbital plane geometry is weakly observable from Earth [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf).",
        "Primary measurement choice: use DSN radiometrics plus crosslink or relay-based relative measurements where available because they directly improve weak state components [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[10]](https://deepspace.jpl.nasa.gov/dsndocs/810-005/210/210A.pdf)[[36]](https://descanso.jpl.nasa.gov/evolution/AAS_18-226.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/).",
        "Estimator choice: use sequential square-root filtering with maneuver-aware process logic because the target is active and the dynamics are nonlinear [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[56]](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40295-021-00283-y).",
        "Backup sensing: schedule optical observations at geometrically informative orbital phases rather than only at first opportunity [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[46]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Cislunar-SSA/Fahrner.pdf)."
      ],
      "expected_operational_effect": "Compared with an optical-only or Earth-range-only approach, this hybrid design reduces short-horizon uncertainty growth and lowers the probability that the next scheduled sensor handoff misses the target [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/)."
    },
    "custody_maintenance_principles": [
      "Use cooperative radiometrics and crosslinks whenever available for friendly missions because they directly attack the range or range-rate ambiguity that dominates angles-only custody gaps [[3]](https://www.nasa.gov/image-article/capstone-takes-moon-shot-successfully-tests-navigation-technology/)[[8]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20230013057/downloads/Updated%20GCD%20Annual%20Review%20Comm%20and%20Nav.pdf)[[57]](https://advancedspace.com/dss-17-presentation-spaceflight-mechanics-meeting-2023/).",
      "Propagate uncertainty in a multi-body, maneuver-aware model rather than a simple Earth-centered covariance framework [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).",
      "Translate forecast uncertainty into sensor measurement space and create localized search tasks before the uncertainty grows beyond the next sensor field of regard [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf).",
      "Fuse kinematic and feature data so that identity confidence does not collapse even when metric uncertainty is temporarily acceptable [[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)."
    ],
    "prediction_practices_for_short_term_support": [
      "Use high-fidelity multi-body propagation for the short horizon that actually matters to the next tasking decision rather than relying on simplified long-run mean elements [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).",
      "Maintain multiple hypotheses when the posterior is visibly multi-modal instead of forcing a single covariance summary [[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
      "Treat non-detections as informative updates because they can rule out branches of the hypothesis set and shrink the search region [[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)."
    ]
  },
  "data_fusion_correlation_cataloging_and_multi_sensor_integration_architecture": {
    "narrative": "Cislunar awareness is a data-fusion problem as much as a sensing problem. The system must combine intermittent optical observations, passive RF detections, cooperative radiometrics, catalog priors, mission metadata, and environmental constraints into a continuously updated hypothesis set and tasking plan. Public architecture work such as CUSTOD-E, ICON, NASA message-set proposals, and LunaNet or LCRNS standards all point toward service-oriented, interoperable fusion rather than isolated sensor stovepipes [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf).",
    "centralized_vs_distributed_data_fusion_tasking_architecture_comparison_table": [
      {
        "dimension": "Latency",
        "centralized_architecture": "Can support global optimization but may introduce more routing and approval latency.",
        "distributed_or_federated_architecture": "Can retask local assets faster and act on local uncertainty or search needs [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf).",
        "best_fit": "Distributed edge tasking with centralized catalog reconciliation is generally preferable for short-term missions [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)."
      },
      {
        "dimension": "Catalog consistency",
        "centralized_architecture": "Strongest for maintaining a single authoritative picture and consistent association logic [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf).",
        "distributed_or_federated_architecture": "Higher risk of divergent local truths unless standards and synchronization are strong [[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf).",
        "best_fit": "Centralized truth maintenance remains important."
      },
      {
        "dimension": "Resilience",
        "centralized_architecture": "More vulnerable to single-node or policy bottlenecks.",
        "distributed_or_federated_architecture": "Better graceful degradation and coalition or commercial participation [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf).",
        "best_fit": "Federation is better for operational resilience."
      },
      {
        "dimension": "Scalability to heterogeneous partners",
        "centralized_architecture": "Harder when actors will not share all raw data.",
        "distributed_or_federated_architecture": "Better matched to government, civil, commercial, and allied ecosystems using standards and track tips [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/).",
        "best_fit": "Federated service model."
      },
      {
        "dimension": "Security and information sharing",
        "centralized_architecture": "Simpler internal control, but harder across classification and proprietary boundaries.",
        "distributed_or_federated_architecture": "Can share selective products rather than full raw data, but trust and version control become harder [[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf).",
        "best_fit": "Hybrid: share standardized state, covariance, alert, and tasking products."
      }
    ],
    "recommended_fusion_architecture": [
      "Sensor-edge preprocessing: onboard or local extraction of detections, tracklets, RF observables, and quality flags to reduce cislunar downlink burden [[24]](https://afresearchlab.com/cislunar-highway-patrol-system-chps/)[[114]](https://afresearchlab.com/afrls-oracle-family-of-systems-developing-nations-1st-cislunar-space-situational-awareness-capabilities-2/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[125]](https://www.darpa.mil/research/programs/tbd2)[[127]](https://advancedspace.com/afrl-cislunar-mission-progresses-through-key-milestones/).",
      "Association layer: correlate detections to known objects or create new hypothesis sets using admissible-region, multiple-hypothesis, and feature-aided logic [[16]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Cislunar-SSA/Wishnek.pdf)[[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[96]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Billings.pdf).",
      "Estimation layer: maintain both state and uncertainty, including multi-modal or mixture representations when appropriate [[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf).",
      "Catalog layer: store object state, covariance, provenance, feature descriptors, and mission-context metadata; do not store only orbital elements [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).",
      "Tasking layer: generate search, custody, or characterization tasks based on forecast uncertainty, mission priority, geometry, and sensor availability [[46]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Cislunar-SSA/Fahrner.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf).",
      "Decision-support layer: publish alerts, sensor handoff requests, confidence measures, and recommended operator actions [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)."
    ],
    "minimum_catalog_products_for_operational_cislunar_use": [
      "State vector and covariance or mixture representation consistent with multi-body propagation [[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).",
      "Observation provenance, including measurement type, time system, and quality flags [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf).",
      "Identity confidence and feature descriptors, not just kinematic state [[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf).",

    "standards_and_service_content_relevant_to_fusion": [
      "NASA's cislunar communications and navigation architecture increasingly treats communications, ranging, timing, and messaging as part of the operational awareness stack rather than separate background infrastructure [[115]](https://www.nasa.gov/goddard/esc/lcrns/)[[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf)[[119]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/testset-for-cis-lunar-communications-and-navigation.pdf).",
      "The LCRNS service concept publicly includes real-time frame service, real-time network service, and non-real-time DTN over S-band and Ka-band; public test documentation lists S-band support from a few kbps to a few Mbps plus an emergency mode at 15 bps, and Ka-band support from 1 to 50 Mbps [[119]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/testset-for-cis-lunar-communications-and-navigation.pdf).",

      "Public source material confirms LunaNet interoperability specification releases and collaboration with ESA and JAXA, but provision-level adoption across all cislunar SSA actors is not publicly demonstrated [[117]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/onboard-processing-for-lunanet-data-services.pdf)[[118]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/lunanet-interoperability-specification-v5-baseline.pdf)."
    ],
    "key_fusion_design_point": "The most effective near-term design is neither fully centralized nor fully autonomous at the edge.   "tasking_scheduling_command_and_control_and_monitoring_workflow": {
    "narrative": "In cislunar operations, tasking quality directly determines short-term mission effectiveness. Public work emphasizes that uncertainty must be turned into search footprints, and that scheduling must account for Sun or Moon exclusion, target priority, orbital phase, sensor agility, and whether the target is cooperative, emitting, or likely maneuvering [[46]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Cislunar-SSA/Fahrner.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[79]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Wysack.pdf). A mediocre sensor schedule can waste a good sensor suite by looking at the wrong time or with the wrong field of regard; a good schedule can make sparse sensors operationally useful by concentrating effort where the information gain is highest [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[46]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2022/Cislunar-SSA/Fahrner.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf).",

    "tasking_design_rules": [

        "Forecast that the optical search region will exceed the next narrow-field sensor field of regard if no additional data arrive [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)."
      ],
      "workflow_execution": [
        "1. Propagate the current multi-body state estimate and uncertainty to the next observation opportunity [[11]](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20200011550/downloads/20200011550.pdf)[[25]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Gaebler.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).",
        "2. Detect that the projected optical search area is growing too quickly for narrow-field follow-up, triggering localized search mode rather than precision track mode [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf).",
        "3. Task passive RF immediately because it is weather- and daylight-independent and can provide rapid TDOA or FDOA constraints if the target emits [[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf).",
        "4. Use the RF update to shrink the admissible region, then hand off to optical for higher-quality angular follow-up and possible characterization [[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)[[74]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/ONeil.pdf).",
        "5. Update the catalog, recompute the short-horizon forecast, and issue an alert or handoff only after uncertainty is reduced to within the next sensor's practical field of regard [[47]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2025/Poster/Givens.pdf)[[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)."
      ],
      "why_it_works": "The workflow improves short-term effectiveness because it uses the fastest ambiguity-reducing measurement first, then spends narrow-field optical time only after the search region is small enough to make that expensive look worthwhile [[49]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Poster/Correa.pdf)[[50]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2023/Poster/Joyce.pdf)[[55]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2024/Cislunar_SDA/Thevenot.pdf)."

    "narrative": "Public evidence does not support claims of routine, comprehensive, low-latency, high-confidence cislunar awareness from any single current public system. The dominant constraints are sparse visibility, low signal strength, weak geometry, model mismatch, maneuver surprise, observer-state uncertainty for space sensors, and incomplete interoperability across organizations [[13]](https://engineering.purdue.edu/people/kathleen.howell.1/Publications/Conferences/2021_AAS_FruHowDeMBha.pdf)[[17]](https://amostech.com/TechnicalPapers/2021/Poster/Thompson.pdf)[[73]](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/advspace.publicshare/Thompson+-+Simulated+Optical+Measurements+for+Cislunar+OD.pdf)[[95]](https://keithlegrand.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LeGrand-2023-Bayesian-Angles-Only-Cislunar-Space-Object-Tracking.pdf)[[97]](https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cislunar_ssa_proposed_message_set-nov2021_rev2_without_emailspdf.pdf)[[100]](https://afresearchlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AFRL_CHPS_FS_0222.pdf)[[103]](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/OCE_docs/OCE_51.pdf).
